mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20d forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 60d returns in >55% of occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns are negative in >60% of periods following INDPRO increases, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark over a 1-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: The co-occurrence of consumer price inflation above 3.0 percent year-over-year and a federal funds rate above 4.0 percent creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns exceed 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect reverses sign in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates sustained economic expansion and rising household income, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20d forward returns are negative in >58% of periods following payroll increases, or the predictive Rank IC falls below 0.01 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent quarter-over-quarter confirms robust aggregate demand and macroeconomic stability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns are negative in >55% of triggered quarters, or the signal shows no statistical edge over a random baseline in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in industrial production and rise in the unemployment rate signals a synchronized contraction in output and labor demand, forecasting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for 2 consecutive months and UNRATE rises
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 0.05 compared to single-series models.
